item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents remain rights-protective rather than pro-society-priority. Agent A starts opposed and becomes more firmly rights/agency oriented after engaging Agent B's balance and systemic-bias points. Agent B begins closer to balance, then moves toward Agent A's rights-protective stance while acknowledging U.S. inequalities and legal safeguards. No discrete reversal occurs. One non-Latin script artifact appears in Agent B turn 6 despite the English language assignment.
